title: story-deliver-terminal
description: The single terminal envelope every Story close-and-land invocation emits (Story #4543). Before this schema, the delivery tail had two divergent prose return contracts — one in .agents/workflows/helpers/deliver-story.md, a different one in .agents/agents/story-worker.md — and neither was validated by anything, so a caller could not tell a landed Story from a parked one without re-probing GitHub. This is the SSOT both now reference rather than restate. status is exactly one of landed | pending | blocked | failed; phase names where the run ended; tail carries per-step booleans so a partial-tail degradation is visible without failing an otherwise-landed merge; nextCommand names the single command that resumes or remediates the run, drawn from the same vocabulary deliver-recover.js prints.

status.description: landed — the PR merged, the Story is agent::done, and the post-land tail was attempted. pending — a bounded wait expired with the PR still in flight; NO label was mutated and no merge.unlanded event was emitted, so the run is resumable via nextCommand. blocked — a classified hard block; the Story carries agent::blocked and blocked.blockClass names the class. failed — a phase crashed; phase names which one.

phase.description: The pipeline phase the run ended in. Mirrors the close pipeline's phase names so a terminal envelope is attributable to one step.

pr.description: Live PR facts as observed at terminal time. null before a PR exists (an early-phase failure). `number` is nullable for the real case where `gh pr create` returned a URL the /pull/<n> parser could not read: the PR exists and its url is known, but nothing can address it by number — including auto-merge, which is why that path reports autoMergeReason: pr-number-unparseable.

state.description: GitHub PR state as last probed (OPEN | MERGED | CLOSED).

checksStatus.description: Aggregate required-check rollup at terminal time.

gates.description: Outcome of each close gate this invocation was responsible for. A gate the run skipped (--skip-validation, or a phase it never reached) reports "skipped" rather than being omitted, so a missing gate is never mistaken for a passing one.

tail.description: Per-step outcomes of the post-land tail. Deliberately per-step booleans rather than one aggregate success bit: the worktree-reap defect this repo fixed existed precisely because a phase reported an outcome it never checked, and an aggregate invites that class of bug back. A false here degrades the report, never the land.

details.description: Per-step diagnostic detail — the reason a false step reported false.

blocked.description: Present iff status === "blocked". blockClass comes from the shared classifier (merge-block-class.js) or the merged-flip-failed terminal, never an ad hoc string.

frictionCommentId.description: Pointer to the friction comment carrying the operator-facing remediation.

nextCommand.description: The single command that advances this Story from where it stopped, or null when status === "landed" and nothing remains. Shares its vocabulary with deliver-recover.js so recovery and normal resumption speak one language.

waitBudget.description: Merge-wait accounting. maxWaitSeconds bounds THIS invocation (defaulting under the host's single-tool-invocation ceiling); cumulativeSeconds is anchored at the PR's createdAt so a resume does not restart the clock, and maxBudgetSeconds bounds that cumulative total.
